message:
fix(tcp): prevent waker/registration resource leak when split halves are dropped

When an OwnedReadHalf or OwnedWriteHalf is dropped while it has a
pending I/O registration, the driver-side waker slot and reactor token
were left dangling until the other half was also dropped. This could
cause the reactor to hold stale waker references and waste kernel-side
event registration slots.

Changes:
- Added TcpStreamInner::clear_waiter_on_drop(interest) that:
  1. Clears the read or write waker for the dropped half
  2. If no waiters remain, drops the registration entirely so
     driver resources are freed immediately
  3. If the other half still has a waiter, narrows the registration
     interest to only the surviving half and updates the combined waker
  4. Falls back to dropping registration if set_interest or
     update_waker fails, ensuring no leaked registrations
- Implemented Drop for OwnedReadHalf (was previously missing)
- Added clear_waiter_on_drop call to OwnedWriteHalf::drop (before
  the existing shutdown_on_drop logic)
- Added two regression tests:
  - dropping_read_half_clears_waiter_and_registration_when_idle:
    verifies both waker and registration are cleaned up when only
    the read half had interest
  - dropping_write_half_clears_waiter_and_keeps_read_interest:
    verifies write waker is cleared but registration persists with
    narrowed READABLE interest for the surviving read half

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
